channel.title: R on Random Research Ruminations
channel.description: Recent content in R on Random Research Ruminations

item.title: Resources for learning (and teaching) statistics
item.description: This is an on-going collection of resources for learning and teaching statistical concepts. This post was last updated on 2020-11-23. Statisticial practices p-hacker app Ned Bicare writes: “I developed an online app that allows to practice creative data analysis and how to polish your p-values. It’s primarily aimed at young researchers who do not have our level of expertise yet, but I guess even old hands might learn one or two new tricks!

item.title: Handling R packages/dependencies with conda.
item.description: Conda for handling R dependencies Every now and then, I stumble across the case that I want to return to some analysis I have been doing some time in the past only to find out that recent developments and package updates totally break my scripts. In the python world, I have been relying on virtualenv for quite some time and it works very well: On the computer I do the work, I create a virtualenv which I enable for development.

item.title: Publishing Bayesian analyses in a medical journal
item.description: New paper: Predictors of response to Web-based cognitive behavioral therapy with face-to-face therapist support for depression: A Bayesian analysis.

item.title: Fixing numerical underflow in WAIC calculation in Stan
item.description: Fixing numerical underflow in WAIC calculation in Stan

item.title: Get Deviance Information Criterion (DIC) when sampling in JAGS
item.description: Get Deviance Information Criterion (DIC) when sampling in JAGS I use the rjags package from R to sample with JAGS. When sampling with the coda.samples() function, there is no way of getting the DIC from these samples (you need to use another run with dic.samples(). When using jags.samples(), it is possible to include deviance and pD in the variable-names array varnames=c('pD', 'deviance', 'var1', ...) out <- jags.samples(model, varnames, n.
